note: Search API Exclude Entity ships a boolean field type/widget/formatter plus a Search API alter_items processor; there is no admin config route. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source for; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with Search API, how does the Search API Exclude Entity module keep an individual node out of a search index? Name the field type and the processor.
evals.expected_output: You add a boolean 'search_api_exclude_entity' field to the bundle; editors tick it; the search_api_exclude_entity_processor (alter_items stage, weight -50) unsets those items during indexing so they never reach the backend.

evals.prompt: I added the Search API Exclude Entity field to my Article type. What else must I do so excluded articles are actually dropped from my Search API index?
evals.expected_output: Enable the 'Search API Exclude Entity' processor on the index (Processors tab) and, in its settings, tick which exclude field(s) control exclusion for that entity type.

evals.prompt: Which permission controls who can toggle the exclude checkbox on entities in the Search API Exclude Entity module?
evals.expected_output: The 'edit search api exclude entity' permission; hook_entity_field_access() forbids editing the search_api_exclude_entity field without it.

evals.prompt: Can I have more than one Search API Exclude Entity exclude field on the same content type, and why would I?
evals.expected_output: Yes. Because it is a normal field you can add several exclude fields on one bundle, e.g. one per index/server, and each index's processor is configured to honor its own field(s).

evals.prompt: What is the admin settings/configure route for the Search API Exclude Entity module?
evals.expected_output: There is none (configure is null). Configuration happens on the Field UI (add the field) and on the Search API index's Processors tab, not a dedicated settings form.

evals.prompt: How do I make the Search API Exclude Entity checkbox appear in the node form's advanced sidebar, and what is the default checkbox label?
evals.expected_output: Keep the widget's 'use_details_container' setting enabled (default true) — it renders the checkbox in a details element in the 'advanced' group. Default label: 'Yes, exclude this entity from the search indexes.'

evals.prompt: Which Search API Exclude Entity submodule excludes items by an arbitrary field value, and which excludes entities whose Metatag robots value is noindex?
evals.expected_output: search_api_exclude_entity_by_field excludes items when an indexed field matches a configured value; search_api_exclude_entity_metatag excludes published entities whose Metatag robots contains noindex.
